examples: Gait Faults: There will be no rolling, paddling, or weaving, when gaited.
The gait of the Boston Terrier is that of a sure footed, straight gaited dog, forelegs and hind legs moving straight ahead in line with perfect rhythm, each step indicating grace and power.
He is quick and light on his feet and when in the show ring should be gaited on a loose lead at a moderately fast trot exhibiting good reach in the forequarters and good drive in the hindquarters.
Stump, even at 10, showed a beautiful topline and gaited serenely.
